title: merremia gracilis
intro: Merremia gracilis, also known as the slender woodrose or five-angled glorybower, is a climbing vine native to tropical Asia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antiviral properties. The health benefits of Merremia gracilis include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate pain and inflammation, and treat skin conditions such as wounds and eczema. In herbal preparations, the leaves and stems of Merremia gracilis can be made into teas, infusions, or decoctions, and the plant's roots can be used to create ointments and salves.
